author: O'Dea 2020
notes: Primary outcome (depressive symptoms) non-significant (d = 0.26, p = .138); intervention primarily targets relationship issues, no depression severity threshold at baseline

author: Smith 2015
notes: Only the post-treatment assessment represents a true randomized controlled comparison. The 3- and 6-month follow-ups included only the Stressbusters group; the waiting-list group was offered Stressbusters after the waiting period, so these later follow-up data cannot be treated as comparative exp-versus-control follow-up outcomes.

author: Wright 2020
notes:
1. Baseline imbalance on primary outcome: BDI exp 18.0 (SD=6.9) vs ctrl 16.0 (SD=6.6); no baseline adjustment in multilevel model.

2. High attrition: 4-month analysed n=42/70 (exp) and 44/69 (ctrl); 12-month analysed n=38/70 (exp) and 37/69 (ctrl). No ITT with imputation; mixed model included only participants with data at ≥1 post-randomisation timepoint.

author: Rohde 2015
notes: K-SADS 16-item composite (study-specific 4-point averaged scale); MI imputed means (20 datasets, Rubin's rules); N reported = randomized N per Table 1 note.

author: Ackerson 1998
notes: Completer data from Table 2. Immediate-treatment group: Time 1 to Time 2 after 4-week bibliotherapy; delayed-treatment group: Time 1 to Time 2 waiting period. No eligible randomized comparative follow-up because delayed group received treatment after Time 2.

author: Stallard 2024
notes: Post M=baseline+ANCOVA adj change; raw post in eTable (unavailable)

author: Fleming 2012
notes: Post M=baseline+change(-14.7/-1.1); SD from change CI

author: O'Dea 2025
notes: Only MMRM estimated marginal means reported; raw post/FU M±SD not available. Differential decline (self-directed vs control): post -1.77 (95%CI -2.98 to -0.56), d=0.35.S
